text:
IV. Stambhana Māraṇa – The Freezing Death Ritual
This is a paralytic form of Māraṇa that doesn’t cause immediate death but gradually immobilizes
the body, halts neural signals, and may result in coma, paralysis, or vegetative state.
Used often in Nāga Tantra, Kāli Kula, and Rudra Yamala traditions, this is known to “freeze the
soul” inside the body—trapping the victim in living torment.
I. Prerequisites & Preparation
Conditions:
Must be done during Paush, Māgha, or Kārtik months (cool, Vāta-Kapha dominant)
Nighttime or Brahma Muhūrta hours
Ideal Nakṣatra: Mṛgaśīrṣa, Ārdrā, or Āśleṣā
Best during Krishna Pakṣa Ashtamī or Chaturdaśī

ol, Vāta-Kapha dominant)
Nighttime or Brahma Muhūrta hours
Ideal Nakṣatra: Mṛgaśīrṣa, Ārdrā, or Āśleṣā
Best during Krishna Pakṣa Ashtamī or Chaturdaśī

Location: Forest shrine, near termite mound or snake pit (symbolic of neural and muscular
system)
Required: Silence + Fast for 12 hours before starting
II. Ingredients List
Material Purpose
Mercury (Pārada) or frozen camphor Represents stillness + nerve poisoning
Ice or water from a corpse-washed body Freezing prāṇa circuit
White mustard & poppy seeds Inducing dullness, sleep, and neuroblock
Sandalwood paste + crushed lotus seeds Stambhana symbolism (sedation and stillness)
Snake skin or molted scale Nerve sheath representation
Victim’s hair or cloth (worn) Bio-resonance
Iron ring from a paralyzed person Transfer of stagnant karma
Black thread dipped in castor oil Nervous channel binding
III. Astro-Magical Alignment

ce
Iron ring from a paralyzed person Transfer of stagnant karma
Black thread dipped in castor oil Nervous channel binding
III. Astro-Magical Alignment

Step Alignment
Tithi Krishna Ashtami or Chaturdashi
Lagna Must fall in the Bādhaka Sthāna (obstructive house)
Day Saturday or Monday
Moon In 8th or 12th from victim’s birth moon
Hora Shani or Budha Hora
IV. Ritual Steps
Step 1: Puppet + Liquid Preparation
Mix sandalwood paste, poppy, lotus seed powder.
Soak black thread in castor oil.
Prepare a vessel of water with corpse-washed water or frozen camphor.
Place the puppet (made of white cloth and victim’s personal item) in front.
Bind the limbs with oiled thread: “यथा बन्धो जीवः सदा स्थिरो भवतु स्वाहा”
Step 2: Mantra & Freezing Bind

e cloth and victim’s personal item) in front.
Bind the limbs with oiled thread: “यथा बन्धो जीवः सदा स्थिरो भवतु स्वाहा”
Step 2: Mantra & Freezing Bind

Place the puppet inside an earthen pot. Add snake skin and iron ring.
Chant:
ॐ ह्ी ींस्तम्भ स्तम्भ मम ररपुीं जडीं कु रु कु रु स्वाहा॥
Om Hrīm Stambha Stambha mama ripum jaḍam kuru kuru svāhā
Repeat 108x holding right palm over the pot.
After every 9th repetition, sprinkle cold water or ice shavings on the puppet.
Optional: Place mercury in a small capsule and bury it beneath the puppet.
Step 3: Ritual Completion – Stambhana Lock
Seal the pot with black wax and chant: “न स्पन्देते, न चलते, न जीवनीं लभते”
Bury the pot under a Peepal or barren tree.
If visible paralysis or coma is desired, snap one limb of the puppet while chanting: “तव गात्रीं पङ गुीं
भवतु, स्थिरीं स्थिरीं स्वाहा”

V. Signs It’s Working
Victim begins experiencing:
Muscular cramps, stiffness
Tremors followed by numbness
Difficulty walking or lifting limbs
Slurred speech, confusion
Eventual neural collapse or vegetative state
VI. Countermeasures
Method Action

numbness
Difficulty walking or lifting limbs
Slurred speech, confusion
Eventual neural collapse or vegetative state
VI. Countermeasures
Method Action

Recovery of buried pot Must be unburied before 21 days and immersed in flowing water
Nila Saraswati Stotra Recite 11x daily with blue lotus incense
External Stambhana antidote ritual Performed by expert Aghori with Panchamukha Rudra
Homa
Nāga Dosha healing Bathe in water infused with Nāga Mantras and Bilva leaves
